_metadata.title: QIF Neurological Condition Mappings
_metadata.description: Neurological conditions mapped to QIF hourglass bands, neural pathways, NISS metrics, and BCI attack vectors. Extends NISS beyond DSM-5-TR (psychiatric) to ICD-10-CM chapters G (nervous system), H (eye/ear), R (symptoms), and N (genitourinary). Diagnostic category references for threat modeling purposes — not diagnostic claims.
_metadata.rationale: A scoring system for neural impact that only measures psychiatric outcomes is like a vulnerability scanner that only checks web applications — it misses the network, the firmware, and the physical layer. The brain is not just a mind. It is also a body interface.
primary_sources: Ropper AH, Samuels MA, Klein JP, Prasad S. Adams and Victor's Principles of Neurology. 12th ed. McGraw Hill, 2023
Kandel ER, et al. Principles of Neural Science. 6th ed. McGraw Hill, 2021
ICD-10-CM 2026. US National Center for Health Statistics
WHO ICD-11, Chapter 08: Diseases of the nervous system
notes: All mappings are outcome category correlations for threat modeling — not diagnostic claims.
ICD-10-CM codes verified against icd10data.com (2026 edition).
BCI attack relevance indicates how the condition could be induced or exacerbated by BCI attack.
Confidence: HIGH = established clinical condition with known neural substrate; MEDIUM = condition established but BCI mechanism inferred; LOW = theoretical.

categories.sensory: Disorders of sensory processing (hearing, vision, vestibular, olfactory, gustatory, somatosensory)
categories.motor: Movement disorders and motor output disruptions beyond existing DSM/ICD-G mappings
categories.autonomic: Disorders of autonomic nervous system regulation
categories.speech_language: Neurological speech and language disorders (distinct from psychiatric)
categories.reflex: Reflex arc disorders and reflexive response disruptions
categories.pain: Neurological pain syndromes and sensitization disorders

conditions.name: Sensorineural hearing loss
conditions.definition: Permanent hearing loss from inner ear (cochlea) or auditory nerve damage.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Cochlear implant firmware attack disabling or corrupting stimulation parameters. Overstimulation can destroy hair cells irreversibly.

conditions.name: Tinnitus
conditions.definition: Phantom auditory perception (ringing, buzzing, hissing) without external sound source.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: TMS pulse pattern targeting auditory cortex can induce chronic tinnitus. Cochlear implant stimulation artifacts produce persistent tonal percepts.

conditions.name: Central auditory processing disorder
conditions.definition: Neurological deficiency in processing auditory information despite normal peripheral hearing. Affects speech comprehension in noise, sound localization.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Thalamo-cortical auditory disruption via TMS or DBS near medial geniculate body. High-stealth: person hears sounds but cannot interpret speech.

conditions.name: Auditory agnosia
conditions.definition: Inability to recognize sounds despite intact peripheral hearing and without aphasia.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Targeted disruption of auditory association cortex (superior temporal gyrus). Sound recognition fails while hearing detection remains intact.

conditions.name: Cortical blindness
conditions.definition: Loss of vision from bilateral damage to primary visual cortex (V1). Pupillary reflexes intact, eyes structurally normal.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Visual cortex prosthesis (Neuralink Blindsight, Orion, CORTIVIS) overstimulation causing excitotoxic damage or cortical spreading depression.

conditions.name: Visual field deficits (hemianopia)
conditions.definition: Loss of vision in one half or quadrant of the visual field from optic tract, LGN, or visual cortex lesion.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: DBS misdirection disrupting lateral geniculate nucleus or optic radiation. TMS targeting posterior visual pathway.

conditions.name: Visual agnosia / Prosopagnosia
conditions.definition: Inability to recognize visually presented objects (agnosia) or faces (prosopagnosia) despite intact visual acuity.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Temporal-occipital junction BCI array disrupting fusiform face area or object-selective regions. High-stealth: victim sees clearly but cannot identify.

conditions.name: Vestibular vertigo (BPPV)
conditions.definition: Episodic vertigo triggered by head position changes. Most common vestibular disorder.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Galvanic Vestibular Stimulation (GVS) directly modulates vestibular afferents. Consumer-accessible hardware (<$200). Known attack vector in TARA.

conditions.name: Vestibular neuritis
conditions.definition: Acute unilateral vestibular dysfunction from inflammation of vestibular nerve. Prolonged severe vertigo with nystagmus.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: High-energy stimulation of vestibular nerve. Cochlear implants share the internal auditory canal with vestibular branch — cross-stimulation possible.

conditions.name: Nystagmus
conditions.definition: Involuntary repetitive eye movements indicating central or vestibular dysfunction.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: GVS-induced vestibular activation directly drives nystagmus. Impairs all vision-dependent BCI use (P300 BCIs require stable visual fixation).

conditions.name: Spatial disorientation / dizziness
conditions.definition: Persistent sensation of movement mismatch between vestibular, visual, and proprioceptive signals.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Repeated GVS establishing pathological internal vestibular model. Effects can persist weeks after stimulation stops.

conditions.name: Trigeminal neuralgia
conditions.definition: Severe unilateral electric shock-like facial pain in CN V distribution. Among the most painful conditions known.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Peripheral nerve stimulator for trigeminal neuralgia: therapeutic-to-attack inversion. Compromised device delivers pain-inducing parameters.

conditions.name: Trigeminal sensory loss
conditions.definition: Reduced or absent sensation in the facial territory of the trigeminal nerve.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: DBS electrode misdirected into medial lemniscus territory at brainstem level.

conditions.name: Anosmia
conditions.definition: Complete loss of smell. Olfaction bypasses thalamus — direct limbic access makes it a unique sensory vulnerability.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Olfactory bulb/piriform cortex BCI disruption. Simultaneously eliminates smell AND disrupts limbic circuit emotional priming.

conditions.name: Parosmia
conditions.definition: Distorted perception of smell — existing odors perceived as different, typically unpleasant.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Olfactory cortex disruption that corrupts rather than silences processing. Qualitative distortion causing food aversion and social withdrawal.

conditions.name: Dysgeusia
conditions.definition: Distorted taste perception via CN VII/IX/X through nucleus tractus solitarius to insular cortex.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Insular cortex BCI (emerging target for interoception/pain) disrupting gustatory cortex as side effect. Food aversion and malnutrition.

conditions.name: Ageusia
conditions.definition: Complete absence of taste perception.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Insular cortex silencing from BCI targeting. Only missing basic sensory modality in existing atlas (gustatory pathway was absent).

conditions.name: Proprioceptive ataxia
conditions.definition: Loss of joint position sense causing ataxic gait, positive Romberg sign. Distinct from cerebellar ataxia.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Spinal cord stimulation disrupting DCML proprioceptive afferents. Motor BCI feedback loop becomes unreliable.

conditions.name: Tactile agnosia (astereognosis)
conditions.definition: Inability to identify objects by touch despite intact tactile sensation. From parietal cortex lesion.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Somatosensory cortex BCI disruption renders sensory feedback from prosthetic limb uninterpretable. Person feels but cannot identify.

conditions.name: Thermoception loss
conditions.definition: Loss of temperature detection (hot/cold) without loss of touch. From spinothalamic tract damage.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: SCS selective A-delta fiber disruption. Temperature sense loss creates burn/frostbite injury risk without warning.

conditions.name: Allodynia / central sensitization
conditions.definition: Pain produced by normally non-painful stimuli. Pathological sensitization of nociceptive processing.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: SCS overstimulation establishing central sensitization. Inverts therapeutic outcome — device designed to reduce pain now causes it.

conditions.name: Complex regional pain syndrome (CRPS)
conditions.definition: Chronic neuropathic pain with allodynia, hyperalgesia, vasomotor changes. Central and peripheral sensitization plus autonomic dysregulation.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Compromised SCS device delivering inappropriate sympathetic-modulating stimulation. Treatment-to-attack inversion.

conditions.name: Dysautonomia
conditions.definition: Generalized failure of autonomic regulation: orthostatic hypotension, heart rate, temperature, sweating, bladder dysfunction.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: DBS near hypothalamus disrupting descending autonomic command. VNS firmware attack dysregulating parasympathetic tone globally.

conditions.name: POTS (postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome)
conditions.definition: Abnormal heart rate increase (>=30 bpm) upon standing with palpitations, lightheadedness, near-syncope.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: VNS inappropriate parasympathetic activation on postural change producing incapacitating orthostatic symptoms on demand.

conditions.name: Neurogenic bladder
conditions.definition: Bladder dysfunction from impaired neural control. Can be hyperreflexic (urge incontinence) or areflexic (retention).
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Sacral nerve stimulator or SCS attack affecting sacral micturition center. Urinary retention is a medical emergency.

conditions.name: Horner's syndrome
conditions.definition: Triad of ptosis, miosis, anhidrosis from disruption of the oculosympathetic pathway.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Known side effect of DBS electrodes near cervical sympathetic pathways. Already listed as disruption effect in atlas.

conditions.name: Neurogenic orthostatic hypotension
conditions.definition: Sustained BP drop upon standing from failed sympathetic vasoconstriction. Syncope and fall risk.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: SCS attack disrupting sympathetic outflow at thoracic level (T1-L2). Produces loss of consciousness upon standing — triggerable, predictable.

conditions.name: Myoclonus
conditions.definition: Sudden, brief, shock-like involuntary muscle jerks from cortical, subcortical, or brainstem origin.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Motor cortex BCI delivering pathological high-frequency bursts mimicking epileptiform discharges. Known neurosurgical observation.

conditions.name: Chorea / Hemiballismus
conditions.definition: Chorea: irregular involuntary movements. Hemiballismus: violent flinging movements from subthalamic nucleus lesion.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: STN-DBS overstimulation producing hemiballismus. Documented DBS complication. Firmware parameter attack on STN target.

conditions.name: Locked-in state
conditions.definition: Complete paralysis of all voluntary muscles except vertical eye movements. Consciousness fully intact. From ventral pontine lesion.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Corticobulbar/corticospinal attack could produce locked-in state. BCI device failure in existing LIS patient removes only communication channel.

conditions.name: Spasticity
conditions.definition: Velocity-dependent increase in tonic stretch reflexes from corticospinal or reticulospinal tract damage.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: SCS incorrect stimulation parameters promoting extensor spasticity. Known complication of epidural spinal stimulation.

conditions.name: Gait disorder
conditions.definition: Abnormality in walking pattern from cerebellar, basal ganglia, corticospinal, or frontal lobe dysfunction.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Vestibulospinal/reticulospinal disruption from rehabilitation BCIs (SCS, epidural stimulation for gait recovery). Fall risk.

conditions.name: Cerebral palsy
conditions.definition: Group of permanent movement disorders from brain injury during development. Major BCI target population.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: BCI attack on CP patient could reverse motor rehabilitation gains. Primary therapeutic indication for motor BCIs.

conditions.name: Persistent vegetative state
conditions.definition: Arousal without awareness. Eyes open but no purposeful response. From diffuse cortical or thalamic injury.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Thalamic relay disruption pushing minimally conscious patient into vegetative state. Consciousness-assessment BCI falsification enabling withdrawal-of-care decisions.

conditions.name: Aphasia
conditions.definition: Acquired language disorder from cortical/subcortical damage. Disrupts production (Broca), comprehension (Wernicke), or both.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Speech BCI (Neuralink, BrainGate) interfaces directly with Broca/Wernicke areas. Attack disrupting cortical language areas produces aphasia — double deprivation.

conditions.name: Dysarthria
conditions.definition: Motor speech disorder from impaired neuromuscular control. Speech present but slurred or unintelligible. Language intact.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Corticobulbar tract disruption from motor cortex BCI near facial motor cortex. Person knows what to say but motor output is corrupted.

conditions.name: Apraxia of speech
conditions.definition: Motor programming disorder — patient knows what to say, has intact speech musculature, but cannot execute learned motor programs for speech.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: SMA/left IFG disruption from motor BCI arrays targeting motor sequence initiation.

conditions.name: Pseudobulbar affect
conditions.definition: Involuntary uncontrollable episodes of laughing or crying incongruent with emotional state. From corticobulbar tract disruption.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Corticobulbar disruption from motor BCI. Already listed as disruption effect in atlas but had no diagnostic code.

conditions.name: Neurological apathy
conditions.definition: Reduction in goal-directed behavior and motivation from ACC, basal ganglia, or frontal lobe dysfunction. Distinct from depression.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: DBS-induced apathy documented in 12-15% of DBS patients. Adversarial DBS parameter modification targeting reward circuit suppression.

conditions.name: Anosognosia
conditions.definition: Lack of awareness of own neurological deficit. Patient is impaired but denies it. From right parietal/frontal lesions.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Dual-disruption attack: produces a deficit AND disrupts awareness systems that would detect it. Highest-stealth scenario — victim cannot report the attack.

conditions.name: Autonomic dysreflexia
conditions.definition: Massive autonomic response (hypertensive crisis) triggered by stimuli below a spinal cord injury. Life-threatening.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Epidural stimulation or FES delivering suprathreshold noxious stimulus to SCI patient. Triggers hypertensive stroke risk.

conditions.name: VOR dysfunction / Oscillopsia
conditions.definition: Failure of vestibulo-ocular reflex causing oscillopsia — world appears to oscillate during head movement.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Vestibular prosthetic desynchronizing VOR. Makes all visual-feedback BCI use impossible.

conditions.name: Bradycardia
conditions.definition: Abnormally slow heart rate (<60 bpm) from excessive parasympathetic (vagal) tone or SA node dysfunction. Can progress to asystole.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Therapeutic parameter inversion — VNS output current/frequency adjusted to maximize cardiac vagal fiber recruitment. Documented adverse event in approved VNS devices.

conditions.name: Tachycardia
conditions.definition: Abnormally rapid heart rate (>100 bpm) from sympathetic activation or parasympathetic withdrawal.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Abrupt VNS cessation in habituated patient or off-target sympathetic activation via current spread from stimulation electrodes.

conditions.name: Syncope and collapse
conditions.definition: Transient loss of consciousness from cerebral hypoperfusion. Vagal-mediated (vasovagal) syncope is the most common mechanism.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Combined vagal/vestibular overstimulation causing vasovagal response. VNS or GVS parameter manipulation triggering reflex syncope.

conditions.name: Mechanical complication of implanted electronic neurostimulator of brain electrode
conditions.definition: Lead migration, electrode fracture, or connector failure in implanted BCI. ICD-10-CM initial encounter code for mechanical device complications.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Firmware manipulation causing mechanical stress on electrode array (e.g., rapid impedance cycling inducing lead fatigue or migration).

conditions.name: Infection and inflammatory reaction due to implanted electronic neurostimulator of brain electrode
conditions.definition: Biofilm establishment or inflammatory response at electrode-tissue interface. ICD-10-CM initial encounter code for infection/inflammatory device complications.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Thermal manipulation via firmware to create conditions favorable for bacterial colonization at the electrode-tissue interface.

conditions.name: Transient cerebral ischemic attack, unspecified
conditions.definition: Transient episode of neurological dysfunction from focal cerebral ischemia without acute infarction. Risk factor for stroke.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Impedance manipulation causing electrode surface charge accumulation promoting thrombogenesis at endovascular electrode surface (e.g., Synchron Stentrode in superior sagittal sinus).

conditions.name: Post-traumatic headache, unspecified, not intractable
conditions.definition: Headache following cranial trauma or neurosurgical intervention. Can result from cortical irritation, meningeal inflammation, or intracranial pressure changes.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Sustained cortical stimulation above comfort threshold causing cortical irritation and post-stimulation headache.

conditions.name: Disorders of acoustic nerve, unspecified ear
conditions.definition: Damage to cranial nerve VIII (vestibulocochlear nerve) causing hearing loss, tinnitus, or vestibular dysfunction.
conditions.bci_attack_vector: Charge density exceedance at cochlear electrode array causing direct CN VIII damage. Vestibular prosthesis malfunction with off-target nerve injury.
